ai_overview.summary: Third-party verifier Kristen from CIC Credit and homeowner Rigoberto Benavidez Diaz contacted Valen Mortgage representative Amanda (agent #1176) for a verification of mortgage. Amanda verified identity using address (5942 Jessup Road, North Chesterfield, VA 23234) and last four SSN (2216) and confirmed permission to release information. She reported the loan transferred from NFM Lending on November 17, 2025; no payment has been received since transfer; the first payment is due January 1st; payment amount is $1,946.85; current balance is $250,400; and the loan is a 30-year fixed. The requested verification was provided and the call concluded with no further questions from the homeowner.

ai_overview.summary: Caller Katrina Koenig (co-borrower) called about options for one spouse to assume the mortgage due to an ongoing divorce. Agent Carla (ID 1383) pulled caller information, explained that assumability depends on the mortgage note and that specific eligibility questions (including whether the divorce must be finalized) must be handled by the assumptions department. Carla provided the assumptions team contact (support@valen.com), offered to have the team send information, and confirmed she forwarded the request so the assumptions team will follow up by email with the application and next steps. No loan number or payoff details were provided on the call.

The agent verified contact information (phone 612-361-7283, email williamandwillanderson32@comcast.net, mailing address 6230 10th Street North, 55128) and explained the servicer does not provide a pre-calculated annual interest total. ai_overview.summary: Customer Chris Marchetti called after receiving a Freddie Mac notice to confirm when his mortgage payments to Valen are due following a loan transfer. Agent Amanda (agent #1176) authenticated the customer, confirmed the payment due date is the 1st of the month with a grace period through the 16th and late fees effective on the 17th, and noted the account is currently in a RESPA grace period so no late fees have been assessed. The agent provided the transferred unpaid principal balance of $503,555.33 and discussed the monthly contractual payment (transcript records the amount inconsistently as '42, 82, 82' / '42,8282'); the customer indicated he will wait for mailed/email communications and then pay online or by phone. The customer confirmed both his and his wife's names are on the account and that either can call.

The agent instructed her to send a written email request to mortgage-insurance@valen.com including her loan number; the back-office team handles PMI eligibility and will respond. The agent provided the unpaid principal balance ($254,705.05) and confirmed the current homeowners insurance carrier as Progressive. The agent also advised that if she obtains a cheaper insurance policy to call back for a new escrow analysis. The agent could not confirm PMI removal eligibility on the call.

ai_overview.summary: Caller Timothy (Tim) Garris contacted Valen Mortgage regarding a credit report entry that indicated his property was "affected by natural or declared disaster," which is blocking a home equity line of credit (HELOC) approval. Tim said he had called multiple times since early November and received an automated email promise to respond that never arrived. Agent Trina (ID 1429) reviewed account notes (including interactions on 11/7 and 11/14 where agents verbally confirmed the mortgage was not modified due to a disaster and a verification of mortgage was sent) and attempted to reach the Insurance Service Center. The Insurance team advised contacting the borrower's insurance provider; however, Trina located prior notes indicating an agent had agreed to provide a letter and confirmation and, unable to resolve the request directly, escalated the matter to the insurance/resolution team. Trina opened/escalated ticket 709-5315 (referencing an earlier ticket 684-9930) and asked that the insurance team email Tim (timmygarris@gmail.com) with a response within 24 hours. Loan number referenced in the call: 2093171816.

ai_overview.summary: Customer Roger Aaron (loan 3699012939) called and spoke with agent Virginia (ID 1202) to limit sharing of his personal information and remove marketing permissions. Agent verified identity (SSN last four 5899, DOB 09/07/1940) and property 218 Heathcote, Winchester, VA 22602. The customer asked to limit affiliate sharing for everyday business purposes (creditworthiness only), to limit affiliates from marketing, to prevent non‑affiliates from marketing, and to remove lending/insurance marketing and text message marketing. The agent confirmed and saved the requested privacy and marketing preference changes. The customer declined a HELOC offer and indicated satisfaction at reaching a representative.

ai_overview.summary: Customer Samaya (Samia) Hennis called Valen Mortgage (agent Wendy, ID 1285) reporting a transfer notice and confusion about where to make mortgage payments. Valen initially could not locate loan number 0035881473; agent contacted Select Portfolio Servicing (SPS) and SPS identified loan 1101107251. Valen then gathered contact information (phone 615-545-9718, email abukero@yahoo.com), verified mailing address (requested mail go to 543 Sunflower Drive, Smyrna, TN 37167), and set up automatic draft from the customer's bank account ending 52093 for $2,036.66 monthly, with the first draft scheduled for Jan 12, 2026 and subsequent drafts on the 9th. The customer also requested email delivery for statements and was connected to a Valen loan officer to discuss refinance options.

ai_overview.summary: Customer Maryam Amadi called Valen Mortgage and spoke with agent Alex (ID 1224) about a recently approved recast. Maryam confirmed she sent $100,000 plus the recast fee; agent verified receipt (payment posted on the 6th) and explained the recast team will send documents that must be signed and notarized (likely requiring the other borrower’s signature) to finalize the new payment. Agent stated the recast paperwork usually takes 3–5 business days to be sent and the new payment will likely start in February or by March. Agent also confirmed Maryam still needs to make the current monthly payment of $7,133.60 (interest is paid in arrears).

ai_overview.summary: Customer Nick Tan Nguyen called and spoke with agent Miriam (ID 1341) about recently changing homeowner insurance to a lower-cost State Farm policy and requesting an escrow analysis to lower monthly payments. Agent confirmed the account reflects the new State Farm policy and noted the escrow account is negative $7,951.70. The customer had canceled the prior Allstate policy and believes a refund check was issued (mentions amounts and a December 26 date) but has not received it. Agent advised the customer to contact the prior carrier to determine whether the refund will be sent to the customer or returned to the mortgage company; if the customer receives the check they should deposit it and make a payment to escrow, then call back to request an escrow analysis (the agent said the analysis cannot be done online). No escrow analysis was run during the call.

ai_overview.summary: Customer Shabana Murugan called Valen Mortgage and spoke with agent Cesar (ID 1390) about an automated mortgage payment that was returned for insufficient funds in January. Shabana confirmed she has paid both January and February payments, had autopay disabled and re-enabled it during the call, and asked for the returned-item fee to be pardoned. The agent confirmed there is no late reporting or late fee (would only occur after 30 days), waived the $20 non-sufficient funds fee, confirmed autopay is scheduled for March 12, provided the new monthly payment amount of $2,170.65 starting in March (taxes +$330, utility taxes +$45, insurance +$210), and verified the homeowner's insurance was paid ($1,730.78).

ai_overview.summary: Borrower Daniel DaCosta called Valen Mortgage (agent Sharla, ID 1152) to confirm that his new homeowner's insurance had been applied and to ask (1) the amount of the unearned premium refund from the prior (lender-placed) insurance and (2) his new monthly mortgage payment now that insurance will not be escrowed. Sharla transferred to the Insurance Service Center where George reviewed loan 554-129-2418 and confirmed the account had a lender-placed insurance (LPI) originally (disbursement recorded as $13,113.08 on May 28, 2025, NatGen LPI) and that Marshall Sterling was the listed carrier once the borrower's policy was received. Documents from the borrower's carrier were received on January 2 and updated January 5; the LPI cancellation is recorded but the refund amount has not yet populated. George estimated the refund should post to escrow about 14 business days from the January 5 notation (approx. January 23) and explained refunds post to escrow first; once the refund appears the borrower should call to request disbursement. Sharla also initiated an escrow analysis (to remove insurance from escrow) and advised the analysis typically takes 3–5 business days and that Valen will follow up by email with the adjusted monthly payment.

ai_overview.summary: Caller Clayton Smith contacted Bell & Mortgage regarding an unpaid real estate tax bill that was collected at closing but not paid after the loan transferred from Mutual of Omaha. Initial agent Javier (ID 1305) verified and transferred the call to Felicia (agent 1076/manager). Felicia reviewed the account, confirmed an escrow balance (~$6,000) and that taxes collected at closing appear not to have been disbursed. The team has opened tickets (originally created Dec 26) and left messages for the closing/title contact (Gabriel at Homeland Title). The customer had emailed documents from a non-verified address; Felicia requested he forward them from the verified email so they are attached to the ticket. Felicia placed a homeowner quick action, escalated the issue to the tax team (and to Bob), and stated the investigation/payment process typically takes 7–14 business days and that valid fees would be covered by the servicer.

ai_overview.summary: Customer Srinivas Bhagawarapu called Valor Mortgage and spoke with agent Maria regarding escrow and property tax charges after a recent loan transfer. Maria stated the loan was transferred on January 2, 2026 and that prior servicing and tax files have not yet been uploaded; she expects the information to be available by Wednesday, January 21, 2026. The customer said he has last year's property tax payment document from the previous owner and wants any escrow adjustment applied so more payment goes to principal. The customer has not made the January payment and asked whether to wait; the agent said he may pay now or wait until the 21st and the customer chose to call back next Wednesday. The customer also asked about refinance/rate downgrade options; the agent attempted a transfer to the lending department but they were busy and provided the direct number 480-745-2319 for the customer to call.

ai_overview.summary: Caller Jamie Anthony spoke with agent Alex (ID 1224) regarding an escrow analysis showing a shortage of $239.71 that was expected to increase future payments. Jamie stated they paid an additional $350 toward escrow with the January payment but still saw an increased minimum payment. The agent reviewed the account, explained that paying the shortage removes a $19.98 spread and that the new payment calculation reflects updated insurance and tax totals divided over 12 months, and said they would send a request to the escrow team to update the monthly payment. The agent advised the customer would see the update in about 3–5 business days. Jamie also reported submitting a web contact on January 13 that received no response; the agent apologized and said they would submit feedback about the missed response.

Agent Barbara (Agent ID 1188) verified the property at 4450 North Trailblazer Place, Meridian, ID 83646 and the email BJCooper818. The agent explained that cashiering will backdate the payments to the actual received date and that any assessed late fees will be waived; if a late fee does not automatically credit, the agent will submit a request to have it removed. The customer acknowledged and thanked the agent.

ai_overview.summary: Customer Lindy Wussow called and spoke with agent Sharla (ID 1152) to request a rerun of her escrow analysis after recently paying an escrow shortage of $1,800.56. Lindy asked whether homeowners insurance can be removed from escrow; agent performed a waiver eligibility scan and sent a DocuSign for an escrow waiver. Agent explained that if insurance is waived Lindy must submit proof of annual payment each year to avoid lender-placed insurance, and noted some accounts may require mandatory escrow if taxes become delinquent. Agent also noted statement dates (statement Jan 5, received Jan 18) and that the escrow shortage takes effect March 1, and said they will run an ad hoc analysis and email Lindy when it's complete.

The agent verified the customer's identity (last four SSN 8107), phone (ending 6195) and email (edw-shei@gmail.com as provided). The customer confirmed she receives bills at 38621 and was satisfied as long as future statements go there.

ai_overview.summary: Caller Chaim (Chaim) Schwartz contacted Valen Mortgage seeking mortgage assistance for a rental property at 40 Jefferson Street, Island North, New York 10930. Agents Barbara (ID 1188) and Genesis (ID 1088) assisted. Account is 235 days past due with a balance of $29,401.44; a notice of intent to foreclose was sent on August 12 and the account needed to be cured by September 16. The borrower reported hardship from reduced income, job losses (lost two jobs), and medical expenses. Genesis advised the borrower to complete and submit a mortgage assistance application (sent by mail and email) and explained required documentation and timelines (initial response 3–5 business days if complete; up to 30 days after submission). The borrower received the application and department contact number (855-716-1340).

ai_overview.summary: Customer Jonathan Smith called Valen Mortgage and spoke with agent Jacob (Agent ID 1230) about a county letter stating his mortgage was paying taxes for a second parcel (customer gave parcel 21-07-15-022A). Agent checked the account and only showed parcel 21-07-15-021 on file. Agent instructed the customer to email a clear photo of the tax bill, along with his loan number (4768687134), to support@valen.com so the tax department can pay the bill and attach the parcel to the file. Agent noted an escrow analysis is scheduled in March and that adding the parcel may change the monthly escrow/payment amount. No parcel update or payment was completed on the call.

ai_overview.summary: Caller identified herself as Kendra Odenwalder (also gave last name as Tandra). She called in response to a letter about recent flooding in Pierce County to report that her property was not damaged. Agent Raven (ID 1328) verified loan number 849-354-6376, contact phone 206-755-7701 and email, and recorded that no property damage or job impact occurred. The agent offered to have a loan officer contact her about home equity; the caller declined but asked a brief question about roof inspection. The agent advised contacting the insurance company for coverage questions and explained a home equity option would be a separate loan and could be handled by lending if desired. The agent recorded the no-damage report and closed the call.

ai_overview.summary: Customer Luis Valenzuela called to request payoff information for his mortgage on 229 Capri Drive ahead of a scheduled closing on the 29th. Agent Jake (ID 1230) verified identity (last four SSN 5332, DOB 06/08/1955) and the customer provided his loan number verbally. The agent confirmed the lender name (Ballon Mortgage) and advised that the title company can call to request the payoff statement; the agent offered to send payoff information if requested. No payoff payoff amount/quote was provided on the call, but the account/loan details and next steps for the title company were given.

ai_overview.summary: Caller Sandra Walker contacted Ballant Mortgages (agent ID 1280) about a notice she received from a third-party (labeled Valen on the notice; agent referenced 'Valet') indicating a special assessment/property tax related to a Bronx meter. The notice showed a parcel number and an amount of $163.23 and referenced her loan number, but Sandra has not received any direct communication from the city and is unsure why she was billed. The agent searched the mortgage account but could not locate supporting documentation or any pending balance and advised Sandra to contact the vendor/city (the 718-595-7000 number on the notice) and possibly Con Edison to resolve or validate the charge.

ai_overview.summary: Agent David from Valley Mortgage spoke with borrower Michelle Forrest (verified) about a mortgage that is 25 days past due. Michelle explained she had been out of work but recently obtained a permanent job and asked to arrange payment for the past-due January payment. The agent offered a six-month repayment plan starting in February and quoted a prospective payment of $5,259.79, then requested gross monthly income (Michelle reported $8,500). Underwriting/system restrictions prevented excluding prior-servicing fees (a little over $5,000), so the agent was unable to set up the repayment plan during the call. Michelle said she would likely pay the January payment by the end of the week.

ai_overview.summary: Agent Tisha (agent ID 1399) from Zalan Mortgage's Mortgage Assistance Department called borrower Star Blank regarding final modification documents for the property at 3008 East Bacchus Road, Mesa, AZ. The borrower had signed and notarized documents and returned them via FedEx, but the servicer received only two pages of the package; underwriting requires the full packet to record the modification and apply the offer. The agent advised they will resend the complete package (expected around Jan 13), Mortgage Connect will provide free notary services when the packet is delivered, and the borrower should continue making monthly payments. The borrower made a trial payment on Dec 31. The account currently shows a delinquency of $7,401.11 and 239 days delinquent. The borrower consented to follow-up calls and said she will notarize the full packet when received.

ai_overview.summary: Customer Ron Gulley called Valo and spoke with agent Emmanuel (Agent ID 1422) to pay an escrow shortage. The shortage amount was $387.84; the agent explained the shortage is spread over 12 months ($32.32/month) and that even if the shortage is paid the monthly payment will not return to the prior $1,284.07 but is projected to be $1,310.92 due to the required escrow cushion and the escrow analysis for the period March 2026–February 2027. The agent instructed the customer that he can pay $387.84 online (selecting additional escrow or principal) and to call back so the agent can submit a ticket to the escrow department to ensure the analysis is applied before March. The customer acknowledged the information and said he would pay online.

ai_overview.summary: Caller Kelly Michaels spoke with agent Danielle (ID 1416) regarding being 148 days past due on her mortgage. Danielle informed Kelly the account had a Notice of Intent to Foreclose dated November 10, 2025, and presented two options: reinstate the loan by paying $7,709.91 or enter a modification trial plan with a payment of $1,248.15. Kelly explained she experienced job loss/hardship and intends to borrow funds to make the first trial payment by Friday to prevent the trial plan from canceling, then make the second and third trial payments in February and March (payments must be made within each month). The agent confirmed the trial timeline and that final modification paperwork would be sent after completion of the third payment.

ai_overview.summary: Juliet Craven (authorized third party for borrower William Travis Craven, property 424 Cedar Creek Road, Blacka, FL 32177) called to catch up on mortgage payments and was concerned about paying online versus mailing a check after a foreclosure notice. Agents Joey (ID 1203) and Lisa (ID 1252) handled the call; Lisa processed a one-time electronic debit of $6,000 from the account ending in 0606 (processing 2–4 business days) and provided a confirmation on the call (confirmation text given verbatim in the call). Lisa noted the account had shown a reinstatement requirement of $7,586.33 but confirmed the notice of intent to foreclose is no longer on the account. She waived one fee of $60.79 as a courtesy, stated total late fees of $1,586.33 remain, and confirmed Juliet may pay those fees over time or apply extra amounts on future payments.

Agent Laura (ID 1321) located the request in the system and confirmed it was sent and delivered on the 20th to micoordination@cct.com (CCT). ai_overview.summary: Agent Kimberly (Valley Mortgage, ID 1317) spoke with Mr. Raphael (property ZIP 33166) about an escrow shortage and an increase in his monthly mortgage payment. The agent explained that taxes and insurance increased, which raised the escrow portion of the payment, and reviewed CFPB rules about dividing annual tax and insurance bills into 12 monthly escrow installments. Mr. Raphael stated he had previously paid amounts toward the shortage and wanted his monthly payment returned to the prior $2,800; the agent explained that the only way the payment would decrease is if the tax or insurance amounts decrease and that paying the shortage will not lower the required monthly payment to the previous level. Mr. Raphael requested a supervisor/manager callback to confirm the explanation; the agent agreed to arrange a callback. No payoff/loan number or balance was provided on the call.

ai_overview.summary: Caller JoLynn from State Farm phoned Ballant Mortgage to verify whether the servicer had received State Farm's bill and declarations (deck) page for loan 110-110-4828 (homeowner Louis Knowles, 320 Hellam Street, Wrightsville, PA 17368). The agent identified as Malcolm (Agent ID 1045) took the information and placed the caller on hold to reach the insurance service center; policy referenced as "9-8-L D V 7-3-0-4-F." The insurance service center rep (Cesar) joined, noted the loan is set up as non-escrow, and said they would assist further, but no explicit confirmation was given on receipt of the insurance documents during the call.

ai_overview.summary: Customer Robin A. Slinger called and spoke with Trina (agent ID 1429) regarding documentation needed by her insurer (Allstate) proving details of her modular/manufactured home (data plate with serial number, manufacture date). Robin said she had spoken earlier to an agent named Emmanuel who purportedly emailed the mortgage note; Trina located a ticket and resent the mortgage note to Robin's portal and confirmed Robin's email (geofandrobin@sbcglobal.net). The mortgage note did not contain the modular data plate information Robin needed. While the agent searched the loan files for manufactured-home documentation, Robin located a document in her possession titled "manufactured home certification" during the call and indicated she would send it to her insurer. No loan number or payoff/balance information was provided in the transcript.

ai_overview.summary: Customer Jody Shaw called Ballant Mortgage and spoke with agent Jacob (Agent ID 1320) about private mortgage insurance (PMI) appearing on her account despite putting 20% down at purchase. Agent reviewed the account, confirmed PMI is being escrowed and noted a current loan-to-value of 82.899% and that prior servicer Mr. Cooper also showed PMI. Agent instructed Jody to contact the mortgage insurance team at mortgage-insurance@valentine.com and confirmed they would send information to her email jodyshaw230@gmail.com. Customer asked if the $1,400 paid toward PMI would be refunded; agent stated a refund would be issued only if the mortgage insurance team determines the charge was incorrect.
